key_insight: The paper shows that a UAS swarm can achieve continuous surveillance with guaranteed correctness by combining decentralized coordination, cyclic role switching, and bounded LTL mission specifications.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical blueprint for certifiable multi-drone surveillance in energy- and communication-limited settings, with decentralized control and finite-time formation convergence.
method: Partition the swarm into stationary anchors and mobile workers with cyclic replacement modes.
Use a DNN-inspired local communication topology that is determined by the reference configuration of newly deployed workers.
Choose worker references by minimizing KL divergence between surveillance-node distribution and induced coverage density, then realize them with a decentralized quadrotor controller.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical simulations demonstrating cyclic team reconfiguration, decentralized topology synthesis, finite-time formation convergence, and certified persistent coverage.
watch_item: The framework depends on accurate local communication, correct mode switching, and faithful model assumptions; real-world disturbances or network failures could weaken the formal guarantees.
paper_title: Distributed Continuous Aerial Surveillance by UAS Swarms Under Formal Mission Specifications
